product_overview: CLIO is a web-based academic writing environment aimed at promoting academic integrity. It integrates a modern rich-text editor with real-time behavioral analytics such as typing speed and paste detection, providing gentle feedback to students and visibility to instructors. It supports professional-grade document exports in DOCX and PDF formats, ensuring consistent formatting across platforms.
core_goals: Provide real-time behavioral integrity feedback during essay composition to encourage honest writing habits.
Offer a distraction-free, exam-friendly writing interface optimized for academic assignments.
Develop a high-fidelity document export pipeline supporting DOCX and PDF formats preserving formatting and embedded images.
Implement lightweight, privacy-aware behavioral analytics focused on typing speed and paste event detection without storing sensitive content.
Enable easy submission and retrieval of essays with validation of typing metrics to support academic integrity assessment.
Design a scalable client-server architecture ensuring cross-platform consistency and performance.
key_features: Rich-text editor supporting headings, lists, text formatting, and inline images.
Integrity status indicator displaying real-time words per minute (WPM) and paste detection feedback with color-coded alerts.
API endpoints for essay submission with validation of WPM and paste counts.
API to retrieve all essay submissions for instructor review.
High-fidelity export pipeline generating DOCX client-side and PDF via server-side conversion ensuring export fidelity.
Optional Spotify integration for focus support during writing sessions.
Responsive UI supporting multiple device types and screen sizes with consistent experience.
user_flow_summary: User accesses the Clio web editor to compose essays using rich-text tools with live integrity feedback.
Typing speed is monitored continuously, and paste events are detected and logged locally for behavioral analytics.
Upon essay completion, the user submits the essay via API which validates WPM and paste counts.
The backend stores submissions securely and makes them available for retrieval via API for instructors.
Users can export their essays as DOCX or PDF preserving formatting and images.
Optional user authentication allows Spotify music playback control integrated within the writing interface.
validation_criteria: Behavioral signals (WPM and paste detection) provide accurate, real-time feedback during essay writing.
Essay submissions enforce required fields and validate WPM and paste counts to detect anomalies.
Exported DOCX and PDF documents maintain over 95% formatting and image fidelity when compared to editor content.
APIs respond correctly with success messages and scores upon valid essay submissions and return all stored submissions on fetch.
User acceptance testing shows a System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 78 or higher indicating good usability.
Performance tests ensure export operations complete within 5 seconds for essays up to 5000 words, maintaining smooth editor responsiveness.

features.name: Submit Essay
features.description: API to submit an essay with validation logic for WPM and paste count.
